sections.label: First screen clarity
sections.dianaTarget: The first screen should make the next school move visible before the student has to prompt a chatbot.
sections.competitorEdge: Quizlet and Gemini feel fast because the first useful action is obvious.

sections.label: Mobile thumb flow
sections.dianaTarget: Mobile should keep Today, Work, Classes, Calendar, and More reachable with no horizontal overflow.
sections.competitorEdge: Quizlet has a familiar mobile rhythm, and Gemini keeps the core chat action close.

sections.label: Unstuck speed
sections.dianaTarget: Diana should get the student to one assignment-specific academic action faster than generic chat.
sections.competitorEdge: Generic chat feels useful when it responds quickly to a typed stuck prompt.

sections.label: Teen voice and control
sections.dianaTarget: Diana should sound direct, private, and student-owned without pressure language.
sections.competitorEdge: Quizlet and Gemini feel consumer-grade instead of school-admin-first.

sections.label: Embedded study loop
sections.dianaTarget: Diana should turn the current assignment into source-linked cards, practice, review, and next support.
sections.competitorEdge: Quizlet wins by turning material into familiar study artifacts fast.

sections.label: Trust without takeover
sections.dianaTarget: Diana should make help boundaries visible without making the experience feel like a warning screen.
sections.competitorEdge: Gemini and Khanmigo keep learning help distinct from simply producing answers.

visualConfidence.label: Actual teen would-love-it confidence
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Repo shows a teen-native identity, Future Path, polished auth entry, and clean responsive proof; live love still requires teen validation.

visualConfidence.label: Public landing first impression
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: The first viewport sells Diana as a student-owned next-move product with one obvious student start path.

visualConfidence.label: Login/signup visual appeal
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Auth pages feel like Diana: student-owned framing, voice/source/privacy cues, Future Mode access, and mobile-safe form hierarchy.

visualConfidence.label: Dashboard focus appeal
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Authenticated screenshots show a polished Today surface with one primary action above the fold.

visualConfidence.label: App shell navigation
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Desktop uses a compact top navigation for the main school moves and a grouped drawer for secondary destinations.

visualConfidence.label: Assignment helper visual learning
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Major subject helpers render as distinct visual learning boards, not generic AI panels.

visualConfidence.label: Study artifacts polish
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Cards, guides, tests, review, and mastery actions are visibly reachable from assignment and note context.

visualConfidence.label: Diana OS cinematic quality
visualConfidence.tenDefinition: Diana OS visibly changes the interaction layer with restrained depth, voice state, proof cues, and reduced-motion safety.

marketGate: Market 10/10 stays gated until 4 of 5 teens prefer Diana on stuck tasks and zero students confuse help with final work.
